ratings.comment: Professor Tannu gives a good high level overview and also can answer deep questions. He makes lectures interesting with real-world examples. Super approachable and helpful during office hours. The course material needs some improvement,  typos on quiz and solutions can be confusing. He is not a bad guy just needs to be bit more polished.

He cares about students learning the material, he is very knowledgeable. Lectures however can be long confusing monologues, but if you read the book in advance and ask him questions you will understand better.
He is all right. He does test hard, but he is helpful if you go to office hours.
The lectures were very very unclear. This shouldn't be a hard course, but his lectures and quizzes made the content confusing.

The professor spends 75 minutes lecturing on 30 minutes of material and explains it worse than the textbook. The project phases are too large and are always due at the same time as another assignment or during a holiday. He has no regard for student time or the course itself. This course is hard only because of how poorly it is taught.

Swamit inherited all of the course material from another professor and doesn't know how to teach the class. He does not know how to lecture the material. He does not know how to explain the concepts when students ask him questions. He does not provide any help on the projects/homework. He has students do way more work than a 3 credit course should.

If you don't have to take a class with this professor, DON'T. Everything is graded very toughly, and the lectures are one of the worst I've ever been to. 

Projects make the class have the workload of a 4-5 credit class even though its 3 credits. Exams are so tough even the professor can't defend some of the answers.
